factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed across segments, with some areas showing growth and others decline.

evidence.subsection: Aerospace

factors.summary: The company is facing some pricing pressures, particularly in certain segments.

evidence.text: Advanced Materials sales decreased 6% (decreased 2% organic) in the quarter ended June 30, 2015 and decreased 9% (decreased 5% organic) in the six months ended June 30, 2015 primarily driven by unfavorable pricing and unplanned plant outages in Resins and Chemicals in the first quarter partially offset by increased volumes in Fluorine Products and Specialty Products.
evidence.subsection: Performance Materials and Technologies

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Pricing pressures and decreased sales due to unfavorable pricing indicate a negative sentiment.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting with declines in certain segments offset by growth in others.

evidence.text: Performance Materials and Technologies (PMT) sales decreased in the quarter and six months ended June 30, 2015 due to decreased organic sales volumes and the unfavorable impact of foreign exchange.
evidence.subsection: Performance Materials and Technologies

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix shifting with some declines and growth suggests a mixed sentiment.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is present but varies by segment, with some showing acceleration and others deceleration.

evidence.text: UOP sales decreased 10% (decreased 8% organic) in the quarter ended June 30, 2015 driven primarily by lower engineering and gas processing revenues due to delays in customer projects, which are expected to continue, and decreased catalyst revenues.
evidence.subsection: Performance Materials and Technologies

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Varied organic growth across segments without a clear trend leads to a neutral sentiment.

factors.summary: Volume trends are mixed, with some segments experiencing growth while others face declines.

evidence.text: Transportation Systems sales decreased 25% (increased 5% organic) in the quarter ended June 30, 2015 and decreased 24% (increased 5% organic) in the six months ended June 30, 2015 primarily due to the Friction Materials divestiture, the unfavorable impact from foreign exchange and lower commercial vehicle production, partially offset by continued growth from new platform launches and higher global gas turbo penetration.
evidence.subsection: Aerospace

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed volume trends with growth and decline across segments result in a neutral sentiment.

factors.summary: End market momentum varies, with some markets like commercial aviation growing while others such as defense and certain industrial segments face challenges.

evidence.text: Commercial Original Equipment sales increased 6% (increased 6% organic) in the quarter ended June 30, 2015 and increased 3% (increased 4% organic) in the six months ended June 30, 2015 primarily driven by higher business and general aviation engine shipments.
evidence.subsection: Aerospace

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed end market momentum with growth in some areas and challenges in others leads to a neutral sentiment.

factors.summary: Input costs are decreasing, particularly due to favorable foreign exchange impacts and productivity improvements.

evidence.text: Cost of products and services sold decreased in the quarter ended June 30, 2015 principally due to a decrease in direct and indirect material costs of approximately $320 million (driven primarily by the favorable impact of foreign exchange and productivity, partially offset by higher sales volume) and a decrease in labor costs of approximately $140 million.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Products and Services Sold

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Decreasing input costs due to favorable foreign exchange and productivity improvements indicate a positive sentiment.

factors.summary: Labor costs are decreasing, contributing to margin improvements.

evidence.text: Cost of products and services sold decreased in the quarter ended June 30, 2015 principally due to a decrease in direct and indirect material costs of approximately $320 million (driven primarily by the favorable impact of foreign exchange and productivity, partially offset by higher sales volume) and a decrease in labor costs of approximately $140 million.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Products and Services Sold

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are decreasing, which is contributing to margin improvements.

factors.summary: Gross margins are increasing due to higher segment margins and favorable foreign exchange impacts.

evidence.text: Gross margin percentage increased in the quarter ended June 30, 2015 primarily due to higher segment gross margin in all business segments (approximately 1.2 percentage point impact) and increased pension income (approximately 0.2 percentage point impact).
evidence.subsection: Cost of Products and Services Sold

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross margins are increasing due to higher segment margins and favorable foreign exchange impacts.

factors.summary: Cost reduction actions are delivering savings, particularly in foreign exchange and productivity improvements.

evidence.text: Cost of products and services sold decreased $221 million and $345 million in the quarter and six months ended June 30, 2015 primarily due to the favorable impacts of inflation and foreign exchange, partially offset by continued investments for growth.
evidence.subsection: Performance Materials and Technologies

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction actions are delivering savings, particularly in foreign exchange and productivity improvements.

factors.summary: Productivity improvements are driving cost reductions and margin expansion.

evidence.text: The increase in operational segment profit is primarily due to the positive impact of productivity net of inflation and higher organic sales volumes partially offset by continued investments for growth.

sentiment.label: positive

factors.summary: Free cash flow decreased due to various factors including changes in working capital and tax payments.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities decreased by $200 million primarily due to (i) a $416 million decrease in accrued liabilities (primarily a $199 million decrease in customer advances and deferred income and $151 million in incentive payments to Aerospace Original Equipment manufacturers) (ii) increased net payments for repositioning and other charges of $81 million (primarily due to the collection of a $130 million asbestos receivable due from one of our insurance carriers in the prior year) and (iii) increased cash tax payments of $60 million, partially offset by a $248 million favorable impact from working capital and increased net income of $206 million.
evidence.subsection: Cash Flow Summary

sentiment.label: negative

factors.summary: Headcount restructuring is ongoing as part of repositioning actions, with expected savings.

evidence.text: Our repositioning actions are expected to generate incremental pretax savings of $100 million to $125 million in 2015 compared with 2014 principally from planned workforce reductions. Cash spending related to our repositioning actions was $50 million in the six months ended June 30, 2015 and was funded through operating cash flows. We expect cash spending for repositioning actions to be approximately $125 million in 2015 and to be funded through operating cash flows.
evidence.subsection: Repositioning and Other Charges
